highlights: want to make an impact on a global scale, come make a difference at Fiserv. Job Title Application Specialist - Analyst Program 2026 Technology Analyst Program The Fiserv Technology Analyst Program is a two-year, early career development experience designed to accelerate your growth in the fintech industry. Through two structured assignments within a specific technology track, analysts gain hands-on experience, build technical and professional skills, and collaborate across teams to solve real business challenges. As a performance-driven company, Fiserv is committed to developing emerging talent and creating opportunities for those who demonstrate initiative, growth, and impact. The program includes formal training, mentorship, and exposure to senior leadership, supporting long-term career development within the organization. Job Title: Application Specialist - Analyst Program What does a successful Application Specialist - Analyst Program do? As the Application Specialist, you will play a meaningful role supporting Fiserv’s Deposit Solutions environment, including Enterprise SCO, Fraud and/or TCM systems. You will work closely with internal channel partners and external clients to troubleshoot technical issues, advise on integration and configuration, and guide clients on regulatory and processing questions. A successful associate applies strong case-management principles, high organization, and a service-oriented mindset to deliver best‑in‑class support for our banking clients. What you will do: Serve as the principal point of contact for external and internal customers regarding training, application queries and issues. Research and diagnose problems to determine resolutions for reported incidents. Escalate complex issues efficiently to Tier 3 support and track remediation through closure. Participate in User Acceptance Testing (UAT) and review product documentation for accuracy and client impact. Provide client input to Product Managers, Business Analysts and Developers regarding product features and enhancements. Analyze incidents and trends to identify opportunities to improve service quality and client satisfaction. Develop and share knowledge articles, runbooks and client-facing documentation to enable team and client success. Support scheduled team hours of 8:00 AM–5:00 PM onsite in Milwaukee, WI and assist with rotating extended support coverage as needed. What you will need to have: Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Management Information Systems, Cybersecurity, Technical Project Management, or other technology degree completed December 2024 or after; 3.0+ GPA and/or equivalent military experience. 0-2 years of professional work experience. Ability to work onsite in Milwaukee, WI (regular business hours with occasional rotation for extended coverage). What would be great to have: 3+ years of experience with check/image processing or fraud systems (for example, X9, Check 21, ImageCash). 3+ years of experience supporting enterprise deposit systems (for example, Enterprise SCO, TCM). 3+ years of experience using ticketing systems such as ServiceNow or JIRA for incident and case management. 3+ years of experience scripting or automating tasks using Python or PowerShell. Our offer A stimulating, international, interdisciplinary, collaborative, and supportive work environment, which emphasizes diversity and inclusion.An environment that promotes personal development.The opportunity to pioneer applications of ML in biomedical sciences.Direct access to VIB’s Data Core services, which provide expert support in data management and high-performance computing, including optimized pipelines and large-scale GPU resources.A competitive salary and benefits package, with relocation support if needed.The chance to make a lasting impact on science, health, and society. About VIB.AI VIB.AI is the artificial intelligence initiative of VIB, designed to accelerate breakthroughs in life sciences by integrating advanced AI methods into research, discovery, and innovation. For more information, visit vib.ai/en. How to apply Please complete the application through the VIB online application tool (https://jobs.vib.be/apply/135914). A complete application file should contain the following documents: Your motivation letter stating career goals, past experience, and how these relate to the current position.Your CV.Contact information of three references. Application deadline: August 31st 2026. For further information, please send an email to pedro.goncalves@kuleuven.be, dmitry.kobak@ugent.be or stein.aerts@kuleuven.be with subject title Inquiry: ML Engineer.

Employer VIB Location Leuven Closing date 19 Aug 2026

## Job Details
...
VIB is a leading life sciences research institute in Flanders, Belgium, with a strong focus on translating basic scientific discoveries into benefits for society. With the launch of VIB.AI, we are expanding our mission to harness the power of artificial intelligence for life sciences research, innovation, and impact.
...
The VIB Center for Neuroscience Leuven (CNL) investigates the mechanisms of brain function and neurological disease across the scales of biology, spanning neurodegeneration, neurodevelopment, synaptic biology, and computational neuroscience. AI and large language models have transformative potential across these research areas.
...
We are now looking for an LLM Engineer in a shared position between VIB.AI's Machine Learning Expertise Unit and the Center for Neuroscience. You will split your time between building institute-wide AI tools and applying them directly to neuroscience research challenges.
...
As an LLM Engineer in this shared position, you will:
...
- Develop and deploy LLM-based pilot projects tailored to neuroscience research at the CNL, applying AI to questions in molecular, cellular and circuit neuroscience, neurodegeneration, neurodevelopment and disease mechanisms.
...
- Build and maintain components of VIB's institutional intelligence layer, including agentic bioinformatics workflows, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) systems, and AI-assisted experimental design tools.
...
- Design, implement, and evaluate LLM-based solutions for scientific text mining, multimodal integration of literature and biological data, and knowledge extraction to support discovery.
...
- Collaborate with wet-lab and computational neuroscience researchers to identify opportunities where generative AI and LLMs can accelerate scientific progress.
...
- Work with the Machine Learning Expertise Unit and VIB's AI Studio to translate pilot projects into practical tools that serve the broader VIB community.
...
- Promote ethical, transparent, and responsible use of LLMs in line with best practices and international standards.
...
- Mine opportunities and data at the center’s advanced technology expertise centers and affiliated central core facilities.
...
- Leverage VIB's Data Core services, including high-performance computing pipelines and large-scale GPU resources, to scale LLM development and deployment.

- Knowledge of modern deep learning frameworks (e.g., PyTorch, JAX, TensorFlow).
- Experience with real-world applications of LLMs in scientific or biomedical contexts is a plus.
